name: Spanakopita (Chantilles)
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the 1/3 cup oil in a large pan and sauté the scallions until soft.
2. Pan-fry the spinach (cook it without adding water, then drain it thoroughly) or salt and rinse it (the latter method is used extensively in Greece, but it is not advisable).
3. Squeeze out excess liquid from the spinach.
4. Add the spinach to the sautéed scallions and stir in the parsley, dill, a small amount of salt, and pepper. Cook gently for 10 minutes, then cool. This can be done in advance and stored in the refrigerator.
5. Using a wooden spoon, stir in the feta cheese and eggs.
6. Butter a baking dish measuring 9 x 12 x 3 inches.
7. Lay 6 filo sheets over the bottom of the dish, brushing each with melted butter.
8. Pour in the spinach filling, then cover with the remaining filo sheets (again, brushing each with butter).
9. Tuck the top filo sheet over the bottom and flute the edges.
10. Score the top 3 filo sheets into square or diamond shapes.
11. Bake in a moderate oven (350°F) for 40 minutes, or until crisp and golden brown.
12. Remove from the oven and let stand for 15 minutes before cutting. Serve warm.
Note: Some popular variations include using nutmeg, substituting mint for dill, using grated cheese instead of feta (adjust salt if the cheese is salty), or adding a small handful of raw long-grain white rice to the filling. You will surely invent your own flavor combinations, which is why spanakopita is so great.
